title: search mode is a Google-SERP first-page discovery (worker-capped at 25, shallow) — not an authenticated LinkedIn search
summary: linkedinProfiles search mode runs a Bright Data Google SERP: it Googles 'site:linkedin.com/in <query>', reads ONLY the first results page (start=0, no pagination), and keeps the /in/ URLs. So: (1) realistic yield is often <10 even when you ask for more; (2) the worker hard-caps max_results at min(n,25) even though the API/Pydantic accepts search_limit up to 50; (3) records are SHALLOW (url+name+headline+snippet, name/headline parsed from the Google title, location best-effort from the snippet) — re-run a result's linkedin_url through profile mode (~$0.003 each) for depth. No LinkedIn account, no proxy. This is a DIFFERENT service from the account/proxy/warmup-gated SpiderPublicLinkedin (linkedinSearch.yaml, Voyager search_people), out of scope here.
references: windmill-scripts/spiderpeople_search.py:244-267 — _extract_profile_summary: name/headline split from Google title, location from snippet
app/schemas/spider_people.py:42-47 — search_limit Field ge=1 le=50 (API accepts 50; worker caps 25)
app/flows/linkedinSearch.yaml + docs/services/SpiderPublicLinkedin/CLAUDE.md — the separate Voyager search service (out of scope)
